checked_arithmetic.claim: Area between y=x and y=x^2 on [0,1] = 1/6
Area between x=y and x=y^2-2 from y=-1 to y=2 = 9/2
checked_arithmetic.work: Intersections: y = y^2 - 2 gives (y-2)(y+1) = 0. Right curve check at y=0: line gives x=0, parabola gives x=-2, line is right. Integral -1 to 2 of (y - (y^2 - 2)) dy = [-y^3/3 + y^2/2 + 2y] from -1 to 2 = (-8/3 + 2 + 4) - (1/3 + 1/2 - 2) = 10/3 + 7/6 = 27/6 = 9/2.
Intersections at 0 and 2. Line on top. Integral 0 to 2 of (2x - x^2) dx = [x^2 - x^3/3] = 4 - 8/3 = 4/3.

adversarial_student.notes: Section 05 (rectangle-with-same-area geometric visual) preempts the 'average value = (f(a) + f(b))/2' misconception. Section 10 ties accumulation to FTC implicitly without overloading the lesson. Section 17 explicitly addresses the highest-frequency error (treating integral of v as total distance).

aggregate.rationale: Zero critical, zero minor across all three reviewers. All six CHA 8.1-8.6 topics covered. All worked arithmetic verified. Proceed to Stage 3 (writer).
